title: Decision requesting clarification on the Prosecutor's Application under Article 58
text:
- […] that the wording of articles 13(a) and 14(1) of the Statute seems to require that the subject matter of the referral consist of events that either occurred before or are still ongoing at the time of its submission to the Prosecutor; (para. 6).

- […] that the same logic appears, with respect to the subject matter of the investigation, in article 53(1)(a) of the Statute, pursuant to which, in deciding whether to initiate an investigation, the Prosecutor shall consider, inter alia, whether the available information "provides a reasonable basis to believe that a crime within the jurisdiction of the Court has been or is being committed"; (para. 7).

- […] since the submission of a situation to the Court, whether by way of referral or as a consequence of the authorisation to a motu proprio investigation, triggers and determines the scope of its jurisdiction according to temporal, territorial and material parameters, the scope of any situation should be identified; (para. 8).

- […] the need for such identification is rooted inter alia in the principle of complementarity as the paramount principle governing the relationship between the Court and national jurisdictions, according to which the Court is meant to complement, rather than replace, national jurisdictions; (para. 9).

- [...].

- […] in light of the aforementioned provisions and principles, as well as of the time elapsed between the submission of the […] and the events mentioned in the Application, it is necessary for the Chamber to determine whether such events are sufficiently linked to the situation of crisis that triggered the […] investigation, so as to fall within its scope and therefore within the Court's jurisdiction; (para. 12).

- [...].

- […] that the issue appears of a fundamental, novel and complex nature so as to make it appropriate to provide the Prosecutor with an opportunity to submit the necessary clarifications; (para. 15).
keywords: art. 13.a RS ; exercise of jurisdiction ; referral of situation to Prosecutor ; referral of situation by State Party ;

art. 53.1.a RS ; investigation by Prosecutor ; initiation of investigation ; reasonable basis to proceed with investigation ; reasonable basis to believe commission of crime within the jurisdiction of the Court ;
